name: Chicago
description: The Windy City — deep-dish pizza, the Cubs, the Bulls, the Bean, and the tallest buildings in North America.

questions.q: Chicago is the largest city in which U.S. state?
Chicago sits on the shore of which Great Lake?
What is the popular nickname of Chicago?
Chicago's NBA team, famous for the Michael Jordan dynasty of the 1990s, is called what?
Which Chicago-based pizza style features a thick crust and inverted cheese-on-top construction?
Which chrome sculpture nicknamed 'The Bean' reflects the skyline in Millennium Park?
Which skyscraper, once the tallest in the world, is now known as the Willis Tower?
Chicago's MLB North Side team plays at Wrigley Field. What is it called?

source.notes: Won the 2016 World Series after a 108-year drought.

questions.q: Chicago's MLB South Side team is called what?
Chicago's elevated downtown commercial district, famously circled by trains, is known by what name?

source.notes: Name comes from the elevated 'L' train tracks that encircle it.

questions.q: The 'Magnificent Mile' refers to a shopping/hotel stretch of which Chicago avenue?

source.notes: North Michigan Avenue between the Chicago River and Oak Street.

questions.q: The 1871 Great Chicago Fire is said — perhaps apocryphally — to have started in a barn owned by whom?

source.notes: Patrick and Catherine O'Leary; the cow-kicks-lantern story is likely newspaper invention.

questions.q: Chicago hosted a world's fair in 1893 to celebrate the 400th anniversary of what event?
choices: The discovery of America by Columbus
The founding of the U.S. Senate

questions.q: What is the main airport serving Chicago, one of the world's busiest?

source.notes: Code ORD; IATA heritage code from its old Orchard Field name.

questions.q: Which Chicago-born 19th-century architectural movement pioneered the early skyscraper?

source.notes: Louis Sullivan, Daniel Burnham, John Root.

questions.q: Chicago's NHL hockey team, a member of the Original Six, is called what?
Chicago's iconic public park beside Lake Michigan, home to Buckingham Fountain, is called what?
Chicago is a major hub of which 20th-century African-American music migration genre?

source.notes: Home of the Chicago Blues style developed by Muddy Waters, Willie Dixon, Howlin' Wolf.

questions.q: Which infamous gangster turned Chicago into the center of Prohibition-era organized crime?

source.notes: Capone's Chicago Outfit ran bootlegging during the 1920s.

questions.q: On February 14, 1929, seven gangsters were killed in the 'St. Valentine's Day Massacre,' famously linked to Capone. In what part of Chicago did it happen?

source.notes: At 2122 N. Clark Street, a North Side garage.

questions.q: Which Chicago-born architect was responsible for Fallingwater and the Robie House in Hyde Park?

source.notes: Wright lived in Oak Park, IL, for decades.

questions.q: The Chicago River's color is famously dyed what on St. Patrick's Day?
